turns.content:
# Handoff — Redistribute the feedback ring: delete `rule-api::ring`, wire edges to owning domains

## Overview
The feedback-api crate extraction landed correctly (crate exists, versioned `FeedbackEntry` schema, store, CLI/MCP/HTTP transports; feedback-api 12 tests + rule-api 70 tests pass, workspace builds). But the *ring* stopped one crate too early: all ring edges were dumped into `rule-api::ring`, forcing a rules crate to depend on spec-api + test-api + session-api + ticket-api. A 2026-07-12 review established that the ring is NOT a module — it is an emergent distributed loop where every domain writes feedback into the feedback-api hub and reacts to outcomes. This session's job: delete the `ring` module and redistribute each edge to its owning domain. Start in the memory-api submodule working directory: [./memory-api](./memory-api).

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `EPIC` → 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c — "[content-materialization][epic] Fill specs, Rust/code policy, and the feedback ring on top of session construction"
- `G-D` → 5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d — "[content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback"
- `DESIGN` → fb6aa078-1f8b-4a76-99bd-3a26190b1208 — "[feedback-api][design] First-class feedback-api crate boundary and unified FeedbackEntry schema v1"
- `INGEST` → 9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226 — "[feedback-api] Event ingestion, metadata normalization, and retention policy"
- `CURATION` → c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9 — "[feedback-api] Core curation surface — URN usage counting + entity ratings (bootstrap gate)"
- `INBOX` → b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76 — "[feedback-api] Feedback inbox, metadata indexing, and deep search"
- `UMBRELLA` → 8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 — "[program][multi-store] Store expansion and operational health program" (lives in the ROOT ticket store, not memory-api)
- `SPEC-FB` → 17fc536c-f08f-4b5f-b6ba-a7bcf1dc4898 — feedback-api/contract/v2 spec
- `BOARD-ENTRY` → bfe509b2-d473-46f5-a022-4b0a853db99e — stale board entry on `G-D`
- `<mem>` → c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api (memory-api ticket/spec/test store workspace)
- `<root>` → c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine (root ticket store workspace, owns `UMBRELLA`)

## Long-Horizon Goal (Epic)
Legitimately close `EPIC` 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c. The feedback ring is the last live blocker. `EPIC` stays `new` until `G-D` genuinely lands with the ring redistributed.

## Findings (grounded this session)
- `rule-api::ring` is a dependency-magnet god-module. [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml) declares feedback-api + spec-api + test-api + session-api + ticket-api — four of those exist only to host ring edges that are not rule concerns.
- Edge-by-edge, rule-api is a bystander in its own module (see [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs)):
  - `recompute_spec_verified_state` (L58): pure spec-api × test-api; zero rule content.
  - `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` (L142): session transcript analyzer; still uses `contains("rule violation")` string heuristics (L156-L160) — burn them.
  - `handle_missing_rule_match` (L223): receives `has_matching_rule: bool` as a parameter — rule-api does no matching here; it just files a ticket.
  - `ingest_frontend_feedback` (L301): pure `store.ingest_rating` — belongs in feedback-api.
- Extraction gates already PASS: dependency direction clean (feedback-api has zero back-deps), schema versioned, transports build, executions recorded passed. Only ring *placement* is wrong.

## Decisions (recorded in tickets this session)
- Redistribution directive written into `DESIGN` description (edge-ownership table) and `G-D` description (redistributed acceptance criteria). Both are authoritative — read them first.
- `INGEST`, `CURATION`, `DESIGN` moved back to `in-implementation`. `G-D` intentionally held at `in-review` (see blocker).

## Blockers
- `G-D` cannot enter `in-implementation`: the store rejects it because its dependency `INBOX` b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76 is only at `ready`, and `INBOX`'s own dependencies are `ready`/`new`. Do NOT cascade-force the whole feedback-api tree forward. `G-D` is a tracker — it progresses when its children do. The actual redistribution work happens under `DESIGN`, `INGEST`, `CURATION`.
- Cross-store edge `G-D` → `UMBRELLA` 8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 cannot be re-added from `<mem>` (entity-not-found; `UMBRELLA` lives in `<root>`). Treat as documentation-only traceability unless cross-workspace edge tooling is added.
- `BOARD-ENTRY` bfe509b2-d473-46f5-a022-4b0a853db99e on `G-D` is STALE (agent `copilot-gpt5-codex`, last heartbeat 2026-07-12 04:03 UTC, TTL 7200s). Owned files: `crates/feedback-api`, `crates/rule-api/src`. Renew or check out before touching those files.

## Key Entities
- [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs) — the module to DELETE; source of the four edges to redistribute.
- [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml) — strip spec-api/test-api/session-api after redistribution.
- [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs) — the hub: `FeedbackEntry`, `FeedbackSource`, `EntityFeedbackStore`; destination for the frontend-ingest edge.
- [./memory-api/crates/spec-api](./memory-api/crates/spec-api) — destination for the execution→verified edge.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api](./memory-api/crates/session-api) — destination for the transcript-mining edge.
- [./memory-api/.spec/specs/17fc536c-f08f-4b5f-b6ba-a7bcf1dc4898/body.md](./memory-api/.spec/specs/17fc536c-f08f-4b5f-b6ba-a7bcf1dc4898/body.md) — SPEC-FB; must describe the ring as an architectural property, not a module; guards wired here.
- [./memory-api/.test/default/specs/val-feedback-api-ring-persistence.json](./memory-api/.test/default/specs/val-feedback-api-ring-persistence.json) — ring-persistence guard; re-scope to per-domain edge coverage.
- Transports (already build): [./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli](./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli), [./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp](./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp), [./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http](./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http).

## Next Actions (execute in order)
1. Claim the board: `board heartbeat` BOARD-ENTRY bfe509b2-d473-46f5-a022-4b0a853db99e or check it out, then re-check-in on `G-D` as the resuming agent with owned files `crates/feedback-api`, `crates/rule-api/src`, `crates/spec-api/src`, `crates/session-api/src`.
2. Move the frontend-ingest edge (`ingest_frontend_feedback`) into feedback-api; delete it from ring.rs.
3. Move `recompute_spec_verified_state` into spec-api (spec×test seam); emit `FeedbackSource::System` via feedback-api.
4. Move `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` into session-api / a transcript analyzer; replace the string heuristics; emit `TranscriptMined` feedback.
5. Reduce `handle_missing_rule_match` so rule-api emits only the no-match signal; relocate ticket+feedback orchestration off rule-api.
6. Delete the now-empty `rule-api::ring` module and strip spec-api/test-api/session-api from [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml).
7. Update SPEC-FB 17fc536c-f08f-4b5f-b6ba-a7bcf1dc4898 to document the ring as an architectural property; re-scope the ring-persistence guard to per-domain edge tests.
8. Validate: `cargo test -p feedback-api -p spec-api -p session-api -p rule-api` and `cargo build` (confirm no dependency cycle); record executions against the guards.
9. Commit deepest-first (memory-api submodule internal commit, excluding Cargo.lock and .session, then parent pointer bumps); then resolve the `G-D` → `UMBRELLA` cross-store edge as documentation-only.

## First Concrete Action
Renew/check out BOARD-ENTRY bfe509b2-d473-46f5-a022-4b0a853db99e and re-check-in on `G-D`, then start Next Action 2 (frontend-ingest edge into feedback-api).

## First Validation Check
After Next Action 2: `cargo build` (workspace) and `cargo test -p feedback-api` — must stay green with the frontend edge relocated.

## Completion Status
1. Claim board — not-started (BOARD-ENTRY is stale; renew before editing owned files)
2. Frontend-ingest edge → feedback-api — not-started
3. execution→verified edge → spec-api — not-started
4. transcript-mining edge → session-api — not-started
5. missing-rule reduced to no-match signal — not-started
6. delete ring module + strip rule-api deps — not-started
7. SPEC-FB updated + guard re-scoped — not-started
8. per-domain + workspace validation — not-started
9. deepest-first commit + cross-store edge decision — not-started
(Grounding decisions in `DESIGN`/`G-D` and moving INGEST/CURATION/DESIGN to in-implementation — completed this session.)

## Definition Of Done
No `ring` module exists in rule-api; each edge lives in its owning domain (spec-api, session-api, feedback-api) with rule-api retaining only the rule-match signal; [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/Cargo.toml) no longer depends on spec-api/test-api/session-api; per-domain edge tests + workspace build pass with no dependency cycle; SPEC-FB documents the ring as a distributed property; commits landed deepest-first. `G-D` then progresses as its children complete, unblocking `EPIC`.

## Board / Session Note
The stale `G-D` board entry must be renewed or checked out before editing `crates/feedback-api` or `crates/rule-api/src` (file ownership held by `copilot-gpt5-codex`). Ticket writes for `G-D`/`DESIGN`/`INGEST`/`CURATION` target the `<mem>` store; the `UMBRELLA` edge decision requires the `<root>` store.

Execute the Next Actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

Starting by reclaiming the stale board lease in the memory-api store and re-checking in with the required file ownership, then I’ll immediately move the frontend-ingest edge from rule-api into feedback-api and run the first validation gate (`cargo build`, `cargo test -p feedback-api`).I’m going to inspect the current draftboard state, clear the stale `G-D` lease if needed, and check in with the requested owned files so edits can proceed without ownership conflicts.

Board lease was successfully released, but check-in reported an existing active entry for the same agent-ticket pair; I’m refreshing the board snapshot and then I’ll expand owned files if required before coding.

I’ve renewed the stale entry and it is active again; next I’m reading the current ring implementation and target crate APIs so I can relocate only the frontend-ingest edge first, then run the required build/tests.

Context scan confirms all ring-edge functions currently live only in rule-api. I’m now checking spec-api and session-api crate boundaries so I can safely move each edge into the right owner without introducing cycles.
Before editing, I’m pulling the authoritative `DESIGN` and `G-D` ticket descriptions and the current spec/guard text so the redistribution edits match the accepted edge-ownership contract exactly.

I’m now applying the core refactor in one pass: create new owning-domain edge modules (feedback/spec/session/ticket), replace rule-api’s ring with a no-match signal seam, remove the old ring exports, and drop the ring-only dependencies from rule-api.

## Verdict — ring redistribution
I keep showing up to these reviews with a flamethrower and you keep handing me a fire extinguisher. This one's clean. Every gate re-verified independently, not taken on faith:

GateBeforeNowEvidence2/3. Ring placement❌ god-module in rule-api✅[ring.rs](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) **deleted** (−311); 5 edges relocated to owning domains (commit `e65522b` stat)5. No dependency cycle✅✅feedback-api is a **pure sink** (zero domain back-deps); spec-api/session-api/ticket-api/rule-api all point *into* it — acyclic DAG6. Real validation (re-ran)✅✅feedback-api **13**, spec-api **68**, session-api, rule-api, `missing_rule` **2** — all green8. Commit discipline⏳✅`e65522b` = 20 scoped files (crates/.spec/.test, **no Cargo.lock/.session**); root `ba1f73df` bumps **only** the [memory-api](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) pointer (1 line)

### The two things I specifically tried to catch you on — and couldn't

1. **The transcript heuristics.** I fully expected `contains("rule violation")` to survive under a new filename. It didn't. [transcript_feedback.rs:44](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) now tokenizes into a `HashSet`, scores rule relevance by [turn_tokens.intersection(rule_keywords).count()](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) with an `overlap < 2` threshold ([transcript_feedback.rs:50](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)), and gates on a token-set `contains_confusion_signal`. That's an actual set-overlap heuristic, not string bingo. Grudging respect.
2. **The no-match seam being fake.** [no_match.rs](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) is genuinely signal-only — `MissingRuleMatchSignal` + `indicates_no_match()` + `emit_missing_rule_match_signal()`, **zero** `TicketStore`/[feedback_store](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) references. The orchestration correctly lives in ticket-api/missing_rule.rs. rule-api's manifest is now down to a single `feedback-api` dep — spec-api/test-api/session-api all shed.

### Splinters (none blocking)

- **One trivial warning:** `unused import: super::*` in a new test module. Sweep it before `G-D` reaches `done` — a clean redistribution shouldn't ship warnings it introduced.
- **Confirm edge #3 has an integration seam.** The no-match signal (rule-api) and the ticket+feedback orchestration (ticket-api) are now decoupled halves, each unit-tested. That's correct architecture, but verify *something* wires signal→orchestration end-to-end so the edge actually fires in production, not just in isolation. Not a regression (the original also needed a caller), just don't let it rot into two islands.
- **`e65522b` is a fat commit** — it bundles the never-committed feedback-api extraction ([lib.rs](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) +834) *with* the redistribution. Cosmetic; the content is coherent.

### Sign-off
Gates 1–9 pass. `G-D` `5d4078fa` is honestly parked at `in-review`, correctly gated by its `INBOX` dependency `b1e9e744` (still `ready`) — the tracker will progress as its children land, which is the state machine working, not a dodge. `EPIC` `3be95a71` stays [new](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html). Cross-store `G-D`→`UMBRELLA` remains documentation-only as agreed. Residual unrelated unstaged changes were correctly left untouched.

This is what the extraction should have been the first time. Clear the one warning, confirm the edge-3 seam wires up, and this track is genuinely done rather than done-in-quotes.

Review complete — the ring redistribution passes all 9 gates on independently re-run evidence. [ring.rs](vscode-file://vscode-app/c:/Users/linus/AppData/Local/Programs/Microsoft%20VS%20Code/fc3def6774/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) is genuinely deleted, the five edges live in their owning domains, rule-api sheds down to a single `feedback-api` dependency, the graph is an acyclic DAG with feedback-api as a pure sink, and every suite is green. The two things I actively tried to catch (string-heuristic slop and a fake no-match seam) both held up. Three non-blocking splinters flagged: one unused-import warning, a request to confirm the edge-3 signal→orchestration seam wires end-to-end, and a note that `e65522b` is a fat extraction+redistribution commit. G-D remains honestly gated at `in-review`; the full verdict with evidence citations is above.

## Verdict — ring redistribution

I keep showing up to these reviews with a flamethrower and you keep handing me a fire extinguisher. This one's clean. Every gate re-verified independently, not taken on faith:

| Gate | Before | Now | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2/3. Ring placement | ❌ god-module in rule-api | ✅ | ring.rs **deleted** (−311); 5 edges relocated to owning domains (commit `e65522b` stat) |
| 5. No dependency cycle | ✅ | ✅ | feedback-api is a **pure sink** (zero domain back-deps); spec-api/session-api/ticket-api/rule-api all point *into* it — acyclic DAG |
| 6. Real validation (re-ran) | ✅ | ✅ | feedback-api **13**, spec-api **68**, session-api, rule-api, `missing_rule` **2** — all green |
| 8. Commit discipline | ⏳ | ✅ | `e65522b` = 20 scoped files (crates/.spec/.test, **no Cargo.lock/.session**); root `ba1f73df` bumps **only** the memory-api pointer (1 line) |

### The two things I specifically tried to catch you on — and couldn't

1. **The transcript heuristics.** I fully expected `contains("rule violation")` to survive under a new filename. It didn't. transcript_feedback.rs now tokenizes into a `HashSet`, scores rule relevance by `turn_tokens.intersection(rule_keywords).count()` with an `overlap < 2` threshold (transcript_feedback.rs), and gates on a token-set `contains_confusion_signal`. That's an actual set-overlap heuristic, not string bingo. Grudging respect.

2. **The no-match seam being fake.** no_match.rs is genuinely signal-only — `MissingRuleMatchSignal` + `indicates_no_match()` + `emit_missing_rule_match_signal()`, **zero** `TicketStore`/`feedback_store` references. The orchestration correctly lives in ticket-api/missing_rule.rs. rule-api's manifest is now down to a single `feedback-api` dep — spec-api/test-api/session-api all shed.

### Splinters (none blocking)

- **One trivial warning:** `unused import: super::*` in a new test module. Sweep it before `G-D` reaches `done` — a clean redistribution shouldn't ship warnings it introduced.
- **Confirm edge #3 has an integration seam.** The no-match signal (rule-api) and the ticket+feedback orchestration (ticket-api) are now decoupled halves, each unit-tested. That's correct architecture, but verify *something* wires signal→orchestration end-to-end so the edge actually fires in production, not just in isolation. Not a regression (the original also needed a caller), just don't let it rot into two islands.
- **`e65522b` is a fat commit** — it bundles the never-committed feedback-api extraction (lib.rs +834) *with* the redistribution. Cosmetic; the content is coherent.

### Sign-off

Gates 1–9 pass. `G-D` `5d4078fa` is honestly parked at `in-review`, correctly gated by its `INBOX` dependency `b1e9e744` (still `ready`) — the tracker will progress as its children land, which is the state machine working, not a dodge. `EPIC` `3be95a71` stays `new`. Cross-store `G-D`→`UMBRELLA` remains documentation-only as agreed. Residual unrelated unstaged changes were correctly left untouched.

This is what the extraction should have been the first time. Clear the one warning, confirm the edge-3 seam wires up, and this track is genuinely done rather than done-in-quotes.  



Review complete — the ring redistribution passes all 9 gates on independently re-run evidence. ring.rs is genuinely deleted, the five edges live in their owning domains, rule-api sheds down to a single `feedback-api` dependency, the graph is an acyclic DAG with feedback-api as a pure sink, and every suite is green. The two things I actively tried to catch (string-heuristic slop and a fake no-match seam) both held up. Three non-blocking splinters flagged: one unused-import warning, a request to confirm the edge-3 signal→orchestration seam wires end-to-end, and a note that `e65522b` is a fat extraction+redistribution commit. G-D remains honestly gated at `in-review`; the full verdict with evidence citations is above.

I’m taking your two follow-ups as actionable work now: I’ll clear the warning source and add a concrete end-to-end seam test for edge #3 that proves a no-match signal path results in ticket creation plus feedback persistence.

I confirmed the seam is currently two islands (signal is emitted nowhere, orchestration is called nowhere). Next I’ll add a lightweight production adapter in the rule tooling layer that consumes the no-match signal and calls ticket-api orchestration, plus an integration test proving that path.

I’ve added a real production relay path in rule-cli for edge #3 and paired tests that exercise signal→ticket+feedback persistence end-to-end; now I’m running focused tests and checking warnings to verify both splinters are closed.
